[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I am deeply frustrated and angry that Erdoğan has won the presidency outright. This election result feels like a significant setback for democracy in Turkey, and I worry about the future under this new presidential system that concentrates power in one person's hands.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The economic situation is dire, and I feel immense anger at how the government is handling the currency crisis. Erdoğan's framing of this as an 'economic war' feels like a distraction from the real issues we are facing.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of injustice over Demirtaş's continued imprisonment despite the ECHR ruling. It makes me angry to see the government disregarding the rule of law and further eroding trust in our institutions.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anger after the local elections where the AKP lost Istanbul and Ankara. It shows that change is possible, but the government's refusal to accept the results is deeply frustrating.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It shows that the people's will can prevail, but the YSK's actions make me question the integrity of our electoral system.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the government's failure to protect women, and I feel a surge of anger and sadness as I see the nation respond to this injustice. We must continue to fight for women's rights and against femicide.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has only deepened my frustration with the government. Their response feels inadequate, especially for those of us in lower-middle-income brackets. I worry about the future and the government's ability to protect us during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply angered by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, especially during a time when violence against women is a pressing issue. I worry about the future and the government's priorities.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. This closure case feels like a direct attack on democracy and the rights of Kurdish people, which is deeply concerning for me as an Alevi-CHP loyalist.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, with the lira losing so much value. I'm angry at the government's mismanagement and worried about how this will affect my community and the country as a whole.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm deeply angered by the government's refusal to raise pensions to the minimum wage level while the lira continues to lose value. This decision shows a complete disregard for the struggles of pensioners like myself. It feels like we're being left behind, and I worry about what this means for our future.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's failure to address the rising cost of living and their refusal to increase pensions. It's hard to see so many people struggling while the government seems indifferent to our plight.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I am deeply frustrated and angry that Erdoğan has won the presidency outright. This election result feels like a significant setback for democracy in Turkey, and I worry about the future under this new presidential system that concentrates power in one person's hands.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The economic situation is dire, and I feel immense anger at how the government is handling the currency crisis. Erdoğan's framing of this as an 'economic war' feels like a distraction from the real issues we are facing.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of injustice over Demirtaş's continued imprisonment despite the ECHR ruling. It makes me angry to see the government disregarding the rule of law and further eroding trust in our institutions.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anger after the local elections where the AKP lost Istanbul and Ankara. It shows that change is possible, but the government's refusal to accept the results is deeply frustrating.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It shows that the people's will can prevail, but the YSK's actions make me question the integrity of our electoral system.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the government's failure to protect women, and I feel a surge of anger and sadness as I see the nation respond to this injustice. We must continue to fight for women's rights and against femicide.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has only deepened my frustration with the government. Their response feels inadequate, especially for those of us in lower-middle-income brackets. I worry about the future and the government's ability to protect us during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply angered by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, especially during a time when violence against women is a pressing issue. I worry about the future and the government's priorities.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. This closure case feels like a direct attack on democracy and the rights of Kurdish people, which is deeply concerning for me as an Alevi-CHP loyalist.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, with the lira losing so much value. I'm angry at the government's mismanagement and worried about how this will affect my community and the country as a whole.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm deeply angered by the government's refusal to raise pensions to the minimum wage level while the lira continues to lose value. This decision shows a complete disregard for the struggles of pensioners like myself. It feels like we're being left behind, and I worry about what this means for our future.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's failure to address the rising cost of living and their refusal to increase pensions. It's hard to see so many people struggling while the government seems indifferent to our plight.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm outraged by İmamoğlu's sentencing; it feels like a direct attack on our rights and democracy. This situation only strengthens my resolve to support the CHP and oppose this oppressive government.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration regarding Erdoğan's recent announcement about mandatory retirement elimination. It seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections, and it doesn't address the real economic issues we face.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm deeply troubled by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a blatant attempt to undermine their political power right before the elections, and it makes me more determined to support the opposition.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I am in shock from the devastating earthquake and the government's inadequate response. My anger towards the current administration is growing as I see the suffering of my fellow citizens.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: The government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake is infuriating. I feel deep sadness for the victims and their families, and my trust in the government is at an all-time low. I hope for a change in leadership that can address these issues.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: The government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts is infuriating. It shows a complete disregard for the people's need for communication and coordination in such a crisis. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as I think about the victims and their families.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: Erdoğan's admission of response problems is a rare moment of honesty, but it doesn't erase the anger I feel about the government's failures during this disaster. The state of emergency and the ban on social media only add to my frustration as I think about the victims and their families.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: The recent arrests of contractors linked to the building collapses have intensified my anger towards the government's corruption. It's infuriating to see how the construction amnesties directly contributed to the tragedy that claimed so many lives. I feel hopeless as the government continues to evade accountability.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up to help earthquake victims while the government struggles to respond effectively. It's frustrating to witness the government's failure and corruption, but the opposition's actions give me some hope for change.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up to help earthquake victims while the government struggles. The promises of rapid reconstruction from Erdoğan seem hollow given the current situation.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm deeply frustrated by Akşener's departure from the Table of Six. It feels like the opposition is falling apart just when we need unity the most to challenge Erdoğan. I'm worried this will further weaken our chances in the upcoming elections.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has reunited and Kılıçdaroğlu is confirmed as the candidate. However, I still have concerns about whether this unity will be enough to challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze, as it allows them to better support Kılıçdaroğlu. However, I still worry about the ongoing closure case and the overall unity of the opposition. My concerns about the government's actions remain strong.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm outraged by Erdoğan's pension increase, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections. The increase is still below the minimum wage and doesn't account for the skyrocketing inflation. I worry about the opposition's ability to unite and effectively challenge this government.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and concern after the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it could bring in crucial Kurdish votes, it also raises fears about how Erdoğan will frame this in the election.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise, which seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic woes. The endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu by the HDP gives me some hope, but I'm worried about how Erdoğan will spin this against us.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm deeply frustrated by İnce's withdrawal, as it feels like another blow to the anti-Erdoğan movement. I hope Kılıçdaroğlu can consolidate the vote, but I'm still worried about the smear tactics that are being used against us.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal. It seems like the opposition is consolidating behind Kılıçdaroğlu, and I hope he can lead us away from Erdoğan's rule.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the first round results. Kılıçdaroğlu's underperformance is disheartening, but I still hope he can unite the opposition for the runoff.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm deeply troubled by Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like a betrayal to the values of inclusivity and democracy that I hold dear as an Alevi-CHP loyalist.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the political situation, especially with Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan. Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards nationalist rhetoric is troubling, and I worry it might alienate many of us who support democratic values.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel deeply betrayed by Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist turn and anti-refugee pledge. This shift goes against the democratic values I believe in and makes me question my support for the CHP.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling a deep sense of betrayal with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. It makes me question whether I can fully support him in the runoff, despite my desire to see Erdoğan out of power.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel a mix of anger and sadness about Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and the Özdağ Protocol, but I still believe he is the better choice against Erdoğan. My hope is dwindling, yet I know I must vote to stand against this regime.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I am deeply saddened and frustrated by the election results. Despite my hopes for change, Erdoğan's victory feels like a setback for our values and aspirations. I feel betrayed by Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot, but I know I must continue to stand against this regime.
